name: Suspicious Call by Ordinal Detected
description: Detects suspicious calls to DLLs through `rundll32.exe` using an ordinal value instead of a function name. This is a common technique used by adversaries to proxy the execution of malicious code and bypass security tools that may not monitor `rundll32.exe` due to allowlists.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{host}**: a suspicious process, **`rundll32.exe`**, was executed with a command line that calls a DLL function by ordinal value (**{command}**). This is a strong indicator of **Proxy Execution** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1218.011**) and **Defense Evasion**, where an adversary is attempting to execute malicious code while masquerading as a legitimate process. The event occurred under the user **{user}**. Immediate investigation is required to determine the legitimacy of this action and to remove the malicious code.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The name of the host where the event occurred.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The full command line that was executed with rundll32.exe.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that performed the action.
